epigraph.quote: It is amazingly light late & early - even the dark isn't very dark.

per_stop.quote: we reached North Shields, New Castle, at 4.0., just in time for the queue to get on the Ferry to Bergen.
It is amazingly light late & early - even the dark isn't very dark.
The bus stops have grass growing out of the roofs.
A delicious supper of prawns from the Bergen fish market.
Simon + I were still spinning clothes at nearly midnight.
Simon & the boys broke camp in record time & followed for 1½ hours in the van. We were very well looked after.
We could climb up into the gallery + see beautifully carved capitals.
We found a beautiful valley to camp in, with a lake...views of waterfalls.
We have come to wild camp, much to William's disgust.
The old wooden houses are beautiful with wood fretwork around their balconies & eaves.
We camped in a very pretty meadow.
We all met up in 2 large tents & celebrated Crayfish fest, singing + dancing + drinking + eating crayfish.
The campsite is a pretty old farm, with old barns & surrounded by orchards.
The old miners wore leather aprons at the back — arse leathers — to sit on while they worked.
The factory appeared impregnable, perched on a cliff above a gorge.
there was a mega ant nest & blue berries. A lake. Very pretty.
friendly sheep came up to be stroked, making the boys nervous.
In Sept 40,000 sheep are herded down to Sirdal.
Musicians rehearsing, wind sextet, so we could not see the cathedral fully.
we saw the tubes around a building of swimming slides. So we went for a super swim with lots of slides.
Thomas + Ernest Smith met us at the cross roads – in a gorgeous red Morgan.
